Yes, the sentence is grammatically correct and clearly communicates the intent of the invitation. However, if you'd like it to be more formal or concise, here's a slightly refined version:

"Your company has been selected as one of the suppliers for the Centric Brands Packaging Optimization Project. To support this initiative, we invite you to a training session on June 8, from 9:30 to 10:30 am, via Teams. The session will be conducted in English and will introduce the Centric Brands Compliance System."
